metadata.subject: English Language Arts
metadata.description: EFP New Media 10 examines how Indigenous creators harness digital platforms and technologies to share First Peoples stories, assert cultural identity, and challenge harmful stereotypes about Indigenous peoples. Students analyze Indigenous digital storytelling, social media activism, digital art forms, and media representation while developing critical awareness of how Indigenous communities are portrayed in mainstream media. Through hands-on projects, students create digital content from Indigenous perspectives, exploring how technology can be a tool for cultural expression, education, and community connection.
metadata.general_description: An exploration of Indigenous digital storytelling, media representation, and the creation of digital content from First Peoples perspectives.

big_ideas.statement: Indigenous creators use digital media and technology to reclaim narratives, challenge stereotypes, and assert First Peoples voices in contemporary culture.
big_ideas.elaboration: Digital platforms provide Indigenous artists, filmmakers, podcasters, and content creators with tools to control their own narratives and counter misrepresentations in mainstream media. Technology becomes a mechanism for cultural sovereignty, allowing First Peoples to directly communicate their stories, values, and experiences to global audiences without gatekeeping by non-Indigenous institutions.

big_ideas.statement: First Peoples digital storytelling adapts traditional narrative forms and cultural knowledge into contemporary media formats.
big_ideas.elaboration: Indigenous creators blend oral storytelling traditions, visual symbolism, and cultural protocols with digital tools like video, podcasts, social media, and interactive platforms. These hybrid approaches honor cultural heritage while making Indigenous knowledge and stories accessible and engaging for contemporary audiences, including Indigenous youth.

big_ideas.statement: Critical media literacy enables understanding of how Indigenous peoples are represented (or misrepresented) in mainstream media and digital spaces.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students develop skills to analyze media messages, identify stereotypes, recognize whose perspectives are centered, and understand the power dynamics in media representation. This critical awareness allows Indigenous and non-Indigenous audiences to consume media more thoughtfully and recognize the impact of media narratives on cultural perceptions and identity.

big_ideas.statement: Social media and digital platforms enable Indigenous communities to organize, educate, and build solidarity around social justice issues and cultural preservation.
big_ideas.elaboration: Indigenous activists, educators, and community members use digital tools for hashtag campaigns, online education, resource sharing, and collective action. These platforms facilitate connections across geographically dispersed Indigenous communities and amplify Indigenous voices in conversations about systemic injustice, environmental protection, and Indigenous rights.

categories.name: Comprehend and Connect
categories.raw: Students analyze digital media content and make connections between Indigenous digital storytelling, cultural values, and contemporary issues.

competencies.raw: Analyze how Indigenous creators use digital media techniques and platforms to tell stories and convey cultural messages
i_can_statements: I can identify digital storytelling techniques used by Indigenous creators (cinematography, editing, sound design, etc.)
I can explain how Indigenous creators adapt oral traditions and cultural knowledge into digital formats
I can analyze how Indigenous artists use visual symbolism, imagery, and multimedia elements to communicate meaning
I can discuss how different digital platforms (video, podcast, social media, interactive) shape Indigenous storytelling

competencies.raw: Critically analyze media representation of Indigenous peoples and identify stereotypes, biases, and power dynamics in mainstream media
i_can_statements: I can identify and describe stereotypes and harmful representations of Indigenous peoples in media
I can analyze whose perspectives are centered or marginalized in mainstream media narratives
I can explain how media representation affects Indigenous identity, public perception, and social outcomes
I can compare Indigenous-created media with mainstream media portrayals and discuss the differences

competencies.raw: Understand how Indigenous communities use digital media for cultural preservation, education, and social activism
i_can_statements: I can identify examples of Indigenous digital initiatives for cultural preservation and language revitalization
I can explain how Indigenous people use social media for education, advocacy, and community building
I can analyze Indigenous hashtag campaigns and digital activism around social justice issues
I can discuss how digital platforms enable Indigenous communities to share knowledge and organize collectively

categories.name: Create and Communicate
categories.raw: Students create and share digital media content from Indigenous perspectives while communicating critical insights about media representation.

competencies.raw: Create digital media content that tells stories from Indigenous perspectives and incorporates cultural elements
i_can_statements: I can plan and create short digital videos or multimedia projects that share Indigenous stories or perspectives
I can incorporate Indigenous storytelling elements, visual symbolism, or cultural protocols in my digital creations
I can use digital tools thoughtfully to communicate a message aligned with Indigenous values and worldviews
I can reflect on how my digital creations represent Indigenous perspectives authentically and respectfully

competencies.raw: Produce written or multimedia analyses that examine media representation and explain the impact of stereotypes
i_can_statements: I can write critical analyses of media content examining how Indigenous peoples are portrayed
I can create presentations or infographics that identify stereotypes and explain their harmful impacts
I can develop multimedia projects that compare Indigenous-created media with mainstream representations
I can communicate my findings about media representation to different audiences effectively

competencies.raw: Design digital projects that use media for cultural education, advocacy, or community engagement
i_can_statements: I can develop social media content that educates audiences about Indigenous cultures or issues
I can create digital resources or campaigns that raise awareness about Indigenous social justice concerns
I can design interactive digital projects that engage audiences with Indigenous knowledge or stories
I can articulate the purpose and intended impact of my digital advocacy or educational projects

areas.name: Indigenous Digital Storytelling Forms
areas.raw: Exploration of various digital media formats used by Indigenous creators, including video, podcasts, social media, interactive media, and virtual platforms
topics: Indigenous-produced films, documentaries, and short videos
Indigenous podcasts and audio storytelling
Social media platforms as storytelling spaces (TikTok, Instagram, YouTube)
Interactive and immersive digital narratives
Indigenous video game design and digital art
Virtual and augmented reality experiences created by Indigenous artists

areas.name: Media Representation and Stereotypes
areas.raw: Critical analysis of how Indigenous peoples are portrayed in mainstream media and the impact of these representations on Indigenous identity and public perception
topics: Historical and contemporary stereotypes of Indigenous peoples in film and television
Absence and misrepresentation in mainstream media
Racist and dehumanizing imagery in media and advertising
Media ownership and decision-making power in representation
Impact of stereotypes on Indigenous self-image and community wellbeing
Analyzing news coverage and political discourse about Indigenous issues

areas.name: Indigenous Digital Activism and Social Media
areas.raw: Examination of how Indigenous communities leverage digital platforms for education, advocacy, activism, and collective organizing around social justice and rights
topics: Indigenous hashtag campaigns and social media movements
Digital activism around Indigenous rights, sovereignty, and decolonization
Social media organizing for environmental protection and land defense
Online education and resource sharing by Indigenous educators
Indigenous-led digital safety and community protection initiatives
Cross-community connections and solidarity-building through digital platforms

areas.name: Cultural Expression and Identity in Digital Media
areas.raw: Analysis of how Indigenous creators use digital media to express cultural identity, preserve traditions, and celebrate Indigenous pride and continuity
topics: Language revitalization and reclamation through digital media
Indigenous youth culture and digital expression of identity
Celebration of Indigenous arts, music, fashion, and creativity online
Digital storytelling as cultural preservation and intergenerational knowledge sharing
Indigenous creative industries and entrepreneurship in digital spaces
Community-centered digital projects and initiatives
